lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਰਸੁ ਭੇਟਿ ਭਏ ਸੇ ਪਾਰਸ ਨਾਨਕ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਰ ਸੰਗਿ ਥੀਏ ॥੪॥੪॥੧੨॥
lines.translit: paaras bhett bhe se paaras naanak har gur sang thee |4|4|12|
lines.english: Touching the philosopher's stone, they themselves becomes the philosopher's stone; they become the companions of the Lord, the Guru. ||4||4||12||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨ ਕਉ ਤਖਤਿ ਮਿਲੈ ਵਡਿਆਈ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੇ ਪਰਧਾਨ ਕੀਏ ॥
lines.translit: jin kau takhat milai vaddiaaee guramukh se paradhaan kee |
lines.english: Those who are blessed with the glory of the Lord's Throne - those Gurmukhs are renowned as supreme.
Basant, Third Mehl, First House, Dho-Thukay:
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:
Throughout the months and the seasons, the Lord is always in bloom.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੇਰਾ ਕਿਨੈ ਨ ਪਾਇਆ ਆਦਿ ਅੰਤੁ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: teraa kinai na paaeaa aad ant |1|
lines.english: No one has found Your beginning or Your end, O Lord. ||1||
He rejuvenates all beings and creatures.
What can I say? I am just a worm.
Those who serve You, Lord,

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਰਮ ਸੁਖ ਪਾਵਹਿ ਆਤਮ ਦੇਵ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: param sukh paaveh aatam dev |1| rahaau |
lines.english: obtain the greatest peace; their souls are so divine. ||1||Pause||
in this way, he crosses over the treacherous world-ocean. ||2||
If the Lord is merciful, then the mortal is allowed to serve Him.
By Guru's Grace, he remains dead while yet alive.
Night and day, he chants the True Name;
The Creator Himself is the Doer, the Cause of all.
The Creator created both poison and nectar.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੰਸਾਰ ਬਿਰਖ ਕਉ ਦੁਇ ਫਲ ਲਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: sansaar birakh kau due fal laae |
lines.english: He attached these two fruits to the world-plant.
He Himself bestows His Ambrosial Naam.
He feeds all as He pleases. ||3||
O Nanak, when He casts His Glance of Grace,
Those who are attuned to the True Lord's Name are happy and exalted.
Thus, the desire for sin and corruption is ended.
The Lord Himself carries out His Own Will. ||4||1||
Basant, Third Mehl:
Take pity on me, O God, Merciful to the meek.
As it pleases His Will, He keeps me. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸੁ ਬਿਨੁ ਅਵਰੁ ਨਹੀ ਮੈ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: tis bin avar nahee mai koe |
lines.english: Without Him, I have no other at all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਹਿ ਨ ਸਕਉ ਦਰਸਨ ਦੇਖੇ ਬਿਨੁ ਸਹਜਿ ਮਿਲਉ ਗੁਰੁ ਮੇਲਿ ਮਿਲਾਏ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: reh na skau darasan dekhe bin sehaj milau gur mel milaae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: I cannot even survive, without the Blessed Vision of His Darshan. But I shall easily unite with the Guru, if He unites me in His Union. ||1||Pause||
The Guru, the Lord, is pleasing to my mind.
The greedy mind is enticed by greed.
The separated ones are reunited, when they are inspired to serve the Guru.
Forgetting the Lord, it regrets and repents in the end.
They are blessed with the Lord's Name - such is the destiny written on their foreheads. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉਣ ਪਾਣੀ ਕੀ ਇਹ ਦੇਹ ਸਰੀਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: paun paanee kee ih deh sareeraa |
lines.english: This body is built of air and water.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ ਦਾਰੂ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh raam naam daaroo gun gaaeaa |
lines.english: The Gurmukh has the Medicine: singing the Glorious Praises of the Lord's Name.
The body is afflicted with the terribly painful illness of egotism.
Granting His Grace, the Guru has cured the illness. ||3||
It is burning in desire, and burning in egotism.
Those whom the Guru protects and saves are very fortunate.
The four evils are the four rivers of fire flowing through the body.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨ ਨਾਨਕ ਉਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਧਾਰੇ ॥੪॥੨॥
lines.translit: jan naanak ur har amrit dhaare |4|2|
lines.english: Servant Nanak enshrines the Ambrosial Name of the Lord in his heart. ||4||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਸੇਵੇ ਸੋ ਹਰਿ ਕਾ ਲੋਗੁ ॥
lines.translit: har seve so har kaa log |
lines.english: One who serves the Lord is the Lord's person.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਚੁ ਸਹਜੁ ਕਦੇ ਨ ਹੋਵੈ ਸੋਗੁ ॥
lines.translit: saach sehaj kade na hovai sog |
lines.english: He dwells in intuitive peace, and never suffers in sorrow.
Basant, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਰਿ ਮਰਿ ਜੰਮਹਿ ਭੀ ਮਰਿ ਜਾਹਿ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: mar mar jameh bhee mar jaeh |1|
lines.english: They die and die again and again, and are reincarnated, only to die once more. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖ ਮੁਏ ਨਾਹੀ ਹਰਿ ਮਨ ਮਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh mue naahee har man maeh |
lines.english: The self-willed manmukhs are dead; the Lord is not within their minds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੇ ਜਨ ਜੀਵੇ ਜਿਨ ਹਰਿ ਮਨ ਮਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: se jan jeeve jin har man maeh |
lines.english: They alone are alive, whose minds are filled with the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਨ ਸੇਵਹਿ ਤੇ ਹਰਿ ਤੇ ਦੂਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: har na seveh te har te door |
lines.english: Those who do not serve the Lord are far away from the Lord.
They contemplate the True Lord, and are absorbed in the True Lord. ||1||Pause||
They wander in foreign lands, with dust thrown on their heads.
The Lord Himself enjoins His humble servants to serve Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਨ ਸਦਾ ਸੁਖੁ ਹੈ ਤਿਲੁ ਨ ਤਮਾਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: tin sadaa sukh hai til na tamaae |2|
lines.english: They live in peace forever, and have no greed at all. ||2||
